title: 'Not talking about some 56-year-old youth': PM Modi takes apparent swipe at Rahul Gandhi
content: Prime Minister Modi lauded India's young space innovators while addressing Parliament. He highlighted Skyroot Aerospace's average team age of twenty-eight years. This achievement marks a significant milestone for India's private space sector. The Prime Minister also discussed India's economic resilience amidst global challenges. He appealed for productive discussions during the Monsoon Session of Parliament.

title: India approves its first dengue vaccine QDENGA after DCGI clearance
content: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral disease reported in more than 125 countries. According to the company, India accounts for nearly one-third of the global dengue burden, with rapid urbanisation, climate change and widespread mosquito breeding continuing to increase the risk of infection across the country. Vaccine QDENGA is given as two injections of 0.5 ml, administered under the skin three months apart.

title: 'Let the noise be there': Rohit Sharma brushes aside retirement talk after Lord's ton
content: Rohit Sharma silenced retirement rumors with a century against England at Lord's. He scored 138 runs, nearly guiding India to a record chase. Sharma stated his focus remains on contributing to the team's success. He pays no attention to outside noise and its presence. His performance keeps him in contention for future matches.

title: 'Feeling very fine': Wangchuk seeks discharge from hospital to join 'Chalo Sansad' march
content: Sonam Wangchuk feels well and seeks temporary hospital release to join a protest march. His supporters question the continued restrictions on his movement and communication. Wangchuk had previously threatened to end his hunger strike if demands were met. Protesters clashed with police near Jantar Mantar while attempting to reach Parliament. Wangchuk was hospitalized after his health deteriorated during his hunger strike.

title: Google DeepMind CEO has a message for those fearing AI job cuts
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis predicts artificial general intelligence could arrive around 2030.  He emphasizes the need for society to prepare for this transformative technology.  Hassabis also highlighted the growing value of human creativity and unique thinking skills.  He proposed a US-led watchdog to regulate advanced AI models before their release.  This body would test frontier AI and coordinate industry slowdowns if risks demand.

title: Lionel Messi exits FIFA World Cup in tears and as football's greatest
content: Messi, head bowed, hands on his hips, watched 19-year-old Lamine Yamal embrace Torres after the winning goal. Moments later, with tears running down his cheeks, he collected his runners-up medal while Spain celebrated amid confetti and fireworks.

title: Lockheed to build low-cost Patriot interceptor using Hit-to-Kill technology - how it works
content: Lockheed Martin introduced a new, cheaper interceptor for the Patriot air defence system. This PAC-3 ACE missile costs less than half of current options. It aims to help allies counter growing drone and missile threats affordably. Initial production could begin within thirty-six months with industry partners. This development addresses rising global conflicts and competition from new weapon makers.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: CJP march: Police lathicharge protesters near Parliament Street; several detained
content: Protesters clashed with police and RAF personnel near Jantar Mantar on Monday.  They attempted to march towards Parliament despite prohibitory orders being in force.  Security was tightened across the New Delhi district for the Monsoon Session.  Chandra Shekhar Azad and Prakash Raj extended support to the agitators.  The CJP remains open to talks on examination reforms with the government.

title: Meet Sally, humanoid robot set to teach AI in New York school
content: Sally, a humanoid robot teacher, is set to debut at a New York high school, providing personalized educational experiences in specialized classes. Designed to be relatable with her realistic appearance and western New York accent, Sally will support both students and teachers with curriculum-aligned learning and translations. Additionally, the school has taken steps to reassure parents and the local community about the integration of artificial intelligence in the classroom.

title: Oracle founder wanted to buy Apple & fire its CEO, but Steve Jobs stopped him
content: Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison once planned to buy Apple for $5 billion and reinstall Steve Jobs as CEO, revealing he had arranged to borrow the entire sum. But Jobs refused the hostile takeover, telling Ellison "I don't need any more money" and choosing the moral high ground. He returned instead via Apple's NeXT acquisition, becoming interim CEO in 1997 and rebuilding Apple into a trillion-dollar giant.

title: Can your builder change project plans after selling your flat? Here's what RERA says
content: RERA protects homebuyers from unauthorized builder changes to sanctioned plans.  Promoters need buyer consent for alterations beyond minor structural necessities.  Changes to common areas require consent from two-thirds of allottees.  Builders remain liable for structural defects for five years after possession.  Homebuyers can seek relief from the Authority for unauthorized changes.

title: Kerala court sentences 61-year-old to death in Nenmara double murder case
content: The quantum of punishment was decided after the sentencing hearing concluded last Tuesday before Judge Kenneth George, who heard detailed submissions from both the prosecution and the defence before reserving the verdict. The prosecution urged the court to impose the death penalty, arguing that the crime fell within the "rarest of rare" category. It told the court that Chenthamara posed a continuing threat to society and cited his remarks after conviction to argue that he had shown no remorse.

title: 'It's all out in the media': Gill denies Rohit Sharma retirement talks
content: Amid speculation over his ODI future, Rohit entered the record books by becoming the first Indian to score an ODI century at Lord's. The 39-year-old made 138 off 110 balls in what was his 19th year in ODI cricket.

title: Jayden Adams' death: Girlfriend dragged into controversy after tragic passing
content: South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend leaving millions of fans in mourning around the world. While initially there were rumors of Jayden Adams committing suicide due to the grief he caried over his grandmother’s death last month, things have now taken a serious turn.

title: Rajasthan: 7-year-old girl raped in Barmer on pretext of showing house; neighbour arrested
content: A seven-year-old girl was allegedly raped in Barmer city on Sunday evening. Police have arrested a neighbor who lured the child away to a deserted place. The minor reached home crying and narrated her ordeal to her family members. The family immediately took the child to the hospital for examination. An SIT has been constituted and the matter is under investigation.

title: 'Ramayana' Part 1 trailer leaked after Pratham Sankalp event in Delhi
content: A leaked one-minute-28-second clip from Ramayana: Part 1 surfaced online after its exclusive preview at the Pratham Sankalp event in New Delhi, prompting producer Namit Malhotra and director Nitesh Tiwari to launch an aggressive anti-piracy takedown ahead of the trailer's official July 24 release. The Delhi event itself was packed with highlights, from Yash addressing the crowd in fluent Hindi to Ranbir Kapoor apologising for a bout of conjunctivitis and touching Sunny Deol's feet and paying an ode to Arun Govil.

title: 'Hurts me in the soul': Argentina coach breaks down after final loss - WATCH
content: Argentina's coach Lionel Scaloni expressed deep sadness after their World Cup final loss to Spain.  He cited unexpected injuries and immense player weariness as contributing factors to the defeat.  Scaloni is now contemplating his future with the national team after his contract expires.  He plans to discuss his decision with Argentina Football Association president Claudio Tapia soon.  Building another successful team again presents a significant challenge for the coach.

title: Dowry dairies: Inside the room where brides prove cost of their abuse
content: Delhi's Special Police Unit for Women and Children handles numerous dowry-related cases daily.  The unit documents evidence like medical records and chat screenshots for legal proceedings.  Women recount demands for cash and valuables from their in-laws after marriage.  Police officers mediate disputes, aiming for reconciliation or separation for victims.  The unit's work highlights the ongoing struggle against dowry harassment in the capital.

title: Gold price prediction today: Why are gold prices under pressure? Check outlook for July 20 week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices continue to trade on a weak note as geopolitical developments keep investors wary, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: 'Hope for productive monsoon session': PM Modi ahead of Parliament proceedings
content: Taunting Rahul Gandhi in a veiled attack, PM Modi praised the succcessful mission of Vikram-1 orbital launch vehicle highlighting how the average age of the young Skyroot team is merely 28 years, unlike a "56-year-old youth."​​ "India's youth have embarked on a new journey into space. I was told that the average age of the entire team working at the 'Skyroot' startup is merely 28 years. Young people like these have accomplished this feat. I am not speaking of a 56-year-old youth," PM Modi said.

title: FIFA chief guides Trump away as US president refuses to leave stage - Watch
content: Spain secured their second World Cup title by defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time. US President Donald Trump stood with the Spanish players during the trophy presentation ceremony. Boos were heard around the stadium as Trump and Infantino walked onto the field. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the match. Trump had previously intervened in a red card controversy during the tournament.

title: Rupee tumbles 12 paise against US dollar as Middle East tensions lift crude prices
content: The Indian Rupee opened lower against the US dollar on Monday. Rising crude oil prices and Middle East tensions fueled dollar demand. Foreign capital outflows from equity markets also weakened the local currency. Indian stock markets traded lower, with Sensex and Nifty declining. India's foreign exchange reserves saw a notable increase in the previous week.

title: Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties
content: In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings.

title: 20-0: How Spain utterly owned Argentina in World Cup final and rendered Messi irrelevant
content: Spain's triumph was built not on moments of brilliance alone but on collective superiority. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled possession, dictated territory and suffocated every avenue Argentina attempted to explore. World Cup finals are often remembered for drama, controversy or moments of individual inspiration. This one will be remembered for something far rarer - complete domination. Spain didn't just beat the defending champions. They made them disappear.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for this week - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Saatvik Green Energy, and Unimech Aerospace - Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk has picked these as the top stocks to buy for the week starting July 20, 2026:

title: DMK MLA arrested over alleged threat against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: Vilathikulam DMK MLA GV Markandeyan was arrested by police after allegedly making threatening and controversial remarks against Tamil Nadu chief minister C. Joseph Vijay during a public meeting in Kovilpatti. His supporters protested the arrest, while police detained him, questioned him, and planned to produce him before a court, triggering political reactions.

title: Sonam Wangchuk sets 3 conditions to end fast ahead of CJP's ‘Chalo Sansad’ march
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk will end his hunger strike on July 20. He has set conditions for ending his protest against education system failures. Wangchuk demands government accountability or parliamentary assurances from leaders. He is currently hospitalized and calls it illegal detention. A march to Parliament is planned for July 20.

title: 'Your tears are our tears, Captain': Heartbroken Messi breaks down after defeat
content: Spain secured their second World Cup title after defeating Argentina in the 2026 final. Lionel Messi's quest for consecutive titles ended in disappointment following a 0-1 extra-time loss. The Argentine Football Association shared an emotional tribute to their captain after the match. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the contest. This defeat concluded a significant era for Argentinian football history.

title: Nolan's 'The Odyssey' starring scores Rs 70 crore debut in India
content: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' has achieved a record-breaking opening weekend in India. The epic fantasy adventure starring Matt Damon crossed seventy crore rupees net. This film has become Nolan's biggest opener in India and globally. 'The Odyssey' earned over two hundred sixty million dollars worldwide in its debut. The original English version has driven most of the film's business.

title: India's bowling lacks bite without Bumrah; middle-over planning all over the place
content: England dominated the third ODI against India at Lord's, amassing a record 387/3, led by a huge opening partnership between Jacob Bethell and Ben Duckett. India's bowlers, missing Jasprit Bumrah, struggled with discipline and conceded many extras. Poor middle-over planning further exposed India's bowling vulnerabilities, raising concerns for their 2027 ODI World Cup preparations.

title: Arizona mass shooting: 10 injured after gunman opens fire in Tucson
content: A mass shooting in crowded downtown Tucson wounded at least ten people early Sunday. Police officers on patrol heard gunfire and responded to the crowded entertainment district. They encountered and shot the suspected gunman, who sustained life-threatening injuries. Nine other adults were shot and taken to hospitals in critical condition.

title: Spain dethrone Argentina! Torres delivers extra-time heartbreak to Messi's 10-man side
content: Spain secured a dramatic FIFA World Cup victory over Argentina. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time, ending the match 1-0. Argentina played with ten men after Enzo Fernandez received a red card. Lionel Messi's final World Cup appearance concluded without a trophy. Spain celebrated their second World Cup title after a long wait.

title: Drought vs development: Can DK Shivakumar evade the ‘SM Krishna trap’?
content: Karnataka faces a potential drought as monsoon rainfall lags significantly behind normal levels. Chief Minister DK Shivakumar is managing this agrarian crisis, echoing his mentor SM Krishna's past struggles.  Krishna's administration faced similar droughts, which opponents used to criticize his urban development focus.  Shivakumar now balances Bengaluru's infrastructure growth with rural distress, a familiar political challenge.  Modern drought management systems offer some advantage, but rainfall deficits remain a political concern.

title: Lionel Messi, Lamine Yamal share emotional hug after Spain's World Cup triumph
content: Spain secured their second FIFA World Cup title after defeating Argentina. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time. Youngster Lamine Yamal shared a memorable moment with Lionel Messi on the pitch. The Spanish team completed a dominant performance throughout the entire match. This victory marked Spain's second time lifting the prestigious World Cup trophy.

title: Kerala govt to pick 33 schemes from 8 departments for Concurrent Evaluation and Monitoring of Schemes
content: Conducted by a team from Finance Department, CEMS is a real-time analysis of programmes and schemes aimed at reporting the actual progress in implementation, in terms of physical and financial outcomes

title: Parliament Monsoon Session LIVE: Repeated adjournments disrupt both Houses; no business conducted so far
content: Opposition parties are protesting for a discussion on question paper leaks and the student protests in Delhi's Jantar Mantar

title: PMK organises signature campaign urging Tamil Nadu to notify extent of Pallikaranai marshland
content: Speaking during the campaign, the PMK president criticised former DMK minister P.K. Sekarbabu, who was in-charge of the CMDA, for not playing the crucial role of being the watchdog in the State for real estate development

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Protesters marching towards Parliament from Sansad Marg stopped by security forces; tear gas shells fired near Press Club of India
content: CJP spokespersons Ashutosh Ranka and Sourav Das to meet Health Minister J.P. Nadda; Sonam Wangchuk wrote to Safdarjung Hospital, saying he wanted to join Parliament March

title: 500 homestay owners applied for registration, says Karnataka Tourism Commissioner
content: The department formulated guidelines following a few unpleasant incidents in the State involving foreign tourists

title: Palakkad double murder case: Convict Chenthamara gets death penalty
content: Palakkad Additional District and Sessions Court - IV holds that the case falls under the ‘rarest of rare’ category. Court also imposes a fine of ₹20 lakh, which it directs to be paid to the orphaned daughters of the victims

title: Hyderabad stays awake as Spain’s FIFA World Cup triumph against Argentina sparks midnight celebrations
content: Fans watch FIFA World Cup final on a giant screen set up at People’s Plaza in Hyderabad. Several licensed bars, clubs and microbreweries too hosted live screenings.

title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike: Delhi HC agrees to hear wife Gitanjali’s plea for shifting him to hospital of choice
content: A Bench of Chief Justice D.K. Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia agreed to list the case today (July 20, 2026) itself at 2.30 p.m.

title: PM Modi says he hopes ‘monsoon and Monsoon Session are productive’
content: The Prime Minister said reforms initiated by his government “have given courage to youth to dare to take commendable steps”

title: The fine art of creating blood donors at home; and resources for those in Chennai seeking to donate blood
content: A leitmotif among blood donation organisations is the increasing difficulty in finding people with “generous veins”; and the solution could strangely be found in the living rooms of households, as illustrated by these two accounts from Chennai of parents inspiring their children to join the cause

title: Chardham Yatra temporarily halted owing to heavy rains in Uttarakhand
content: The Yatra has been halted as a precautionary measure in view of the red and orange alert warning of heavy rainfall

title: Vilathikulam DMK MLA arrested for remarks against T.N. CM Vijay
content: In a post on X, the DMK’s deputy general secretary Kanimozhi Karunanidhi strongly condemned the TVK government’s action on the MLA and termed it “high-handed”

title: Himachal Pradesh on high alert as red alert issued for heavy rains; all schools closed in three districts
content: The Met office has issued a red warning for heavy rains in Chamba, Kangra, Mandi and Sirmaur districts for July 20 and 21

title: What’s the agenda for the Monsoon session | Explained
content: The government has listed five new Bills for the Monsoon Session of Parliament; The government has neither formally confirmed nor denied reports that it may bring back a revised version of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill during the Monsoon Session

title: CPI(M) opposes introduction of Bill to give Vande Mataram legal protection, calls it unconstitutional
content: John Brittas filed a notice under Rule 67 opposing the introduction of the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026, arguing it breaches the constitutional settlement on the National Song’s status

title: Six killed as truck collides with car on Samruddhi Expressway in Maharashtra's Wardha
content: The two vehicles caught fire after the accident; four car passengers and two occupants of the truck, including its driver, died in the accident

title: Kerala Health Minister K. Muraleedharan alleges vested interests behind row over food packet distribution at MCH
content: Rejecting allegations that govt had banned distribution of food packets at hospitals, Minister clarifies that the decision is confined to Alappuzha MCH and is aimed at removing encroachment on govt land

title: As BSF mulls 90-km Sundarbans fence, security clashes with ecology
content: The BSF is studying a 90-km stretch of mangrove forests in the Indo-Bangladesh border to erect fences to deter infiltration

title: Visiting Guwahati? This leaf-to-cup tea experience is worth the detour
content: Just 40 minutes from central Guwahati, Amchong Tea Estate offers visitors estate tours, tea tastings, off-road adventures and a glimpse into Assam’s rich tea heritage

title: Sonam Wangchuk calls July 20 Parliament march India's 'second freedom movement'
content: The note, shared on X through Mr. Wangchuk's account, read: "20th July. India's second freedom movement. Freedom from injustice (like paper leaks). Freedom from fear [my illegal detention]."

title: Rally in Vijayapura in support of Sonam Wangchuk’s fast over NEET paper leak, demand for resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan
content: The protestors condemned the police action against the educationist and social activist, and sought his immediate discharge from the hospital

title: Parliament session, CJP's rally plan key reasons for Wangchuk's removal from protest site
content: Security agencies were also apprehensive of law and order situation in the national capital in view of CJP’s proposed rally

title: Kannur BDS student death: Prime accused Dr. Ram arrested in Karnataka after weeks on the run
content: Former Kannur college faculty member was absconding since Supreme Court rejected his anticipatory bail plea

title: Pleas filed in Delhi HC against Sonam Wangchuk’s detention
content: The climate activist and several others were detained while marching to the capital to demand sixth schedule status for Ladakh

title: CIC directs IITs to form high-level committees to curb student deaths by suicides
content: It says information on panels must be proactively disclosed on the institutes’ official websites. The direction came while disposing of appeals filed by an IIT-Kanpur alumnus, who is the founder of a non-governmental organisation engaged in student rehabilitation and mental health counselling

title: NEET-UG: NTA warns against fake, AI-generated OMR sheets; legal action may follow
content: The warning comes after the NTA reviewed several complaints regarding differences between candidates' expected and declared NEET 2026 scores

title: Sharad Pawar’s next move? The inside story of Maharashtra’s political drama | Pulse Maharashtra
content:
The political drama in Maharashtra refuses to die down! From secret late-night meetings between CM Devendra Fadnavis and NCP leaders to Eknath Shinde’s sudden Delhi visit, the state’s political alignments are shifting fast.

In this episode of Pulse Maharashtra, Vinaya Deshpande Pandit breaks down the inside story: Is Sharad Pawar’s party mulling over options to support the NDA on the crucial Delimitation Bill? What are the 3 options in front of the NCP-SP? And how did Eknath Shinde’s “Operation Tiger” decimate Uddhav Thackeray’s legislative power?

Watch the full analysis to understand the unpredictable number game that could change the Parliament’s landscape.

title: Heavy downpour slows search and rescue efforts in flood-hit J&K; death toll reaches 12
content: Police, Army, SDRF and Civil Defence personnel and local volunteers continued rescue efforts despite the adverse weather

title: Viral Kumbh Mela woman seeks police protection again in Kerala
content: In her complaint, she has reportedly accused her father of taking away her passport and withdrawing nearly ₹10 lakh from her bank account

title: Neha, a prominent face of the CJP protests, presses on with hunger strike
content: The All India Students’ Association’s national president and two of her colleagues continue the hunger strike demanding reforms in education. She vows to continue the fight to remind people that ‘the divisive politics that the BJP has taught’ is not their legacy

title: Crowd swells at CJP protest site ahead of Parliament march
content: Crowds continued to pour into the CJP's protest site at Jantar Mantar through the day as preparations gathered pace for Monday's march to Parliament

title: Govt. lists five Bills for Monsoon Session, no mention of delimitation legislation
content: Government to table Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill that will make the intentional insult of, or disruption during, the singing of Vande Mataram a punishable offence

title: Jantar Mantar protest highlights: Wangchuk will end fast if Parliament session focuses on education accountability, says Gitanjali Angmo
content: The Delhi Police say ‘no permission sought or granted’ for July 20 Parliament protest march and Section 163 is currently in effect in the New Delhi district

title: The E20 transition: fuel for debate
content: As car and bike owners report clogged injectors, damaged fuel pumps, and contaminated petrol across the country, a look at competing claims on what is driving the complaints, and whether ethanol is really to blame

title: Curbing talent exodus: It’s deja vu for Indian Space Research Organisation
content: In the early 1990s, the information technology boom and campus recruitments dealt a blow to ISRO which was then on an expansion mode

title: India advises citizens to postpone travel to Iran amid rising instability
content: It advised Indians already in Iran to consider temporarily exiting the country using available flight options

title: ‘There was no need for Govt. intervention’: Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali on Delhi HC refusing interim relief on hospital transfer
content: Gitanjali Angmo said the PIL was “wrongly interpreted” and that they will file another petition on July 20

title: Thousands gather at Jantar Mantar ahead of CJP’s march to Parliament
content: The Cockroach Janta Party’s designated protest site overflowed with unprecedented crowds following activist Sonam Wangchuk’s forced hospitalisation

title: Maharashtra panel to study ST benefits after Christian conversion allegations
content: The notice stated that Christian religious leaders, missionaries, and their institutions in Navapur taluka were using inducements to compel tribals and non-tribals to convert

title: Srinagar’s Vichar Nag temple sees puja again, L-G says ‘revival serves as a historic bridge’
content: Scores of Kashmiri Pandits attended the ceremony in an area that once hosted a number of Pandit families who had to leave their homeland due to militancy in the 1990s

title: Maharashtra govt allows hotels, restaurants, bars to remain open till 4 a.m. for FIFA World Cup final
content: The Home Department issued the directive onJuly 18) following a request from the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI), Mumbai, which sought extension of operating hours for hospitality establishments on the night of the final

title: U.K.'s Burnham drops digital ID scheme to prioritise cost of living, say allies
content: Dubbed the ‘King of the North’ for his work as the mayor of Greater Manchester in northwestern England, Burnham will replace Keir Starmer ‌as Prime Minister on July 20, when he says he will also unveil his cabinet team of top Ministers

title: Geo-tagging of State’s forest locations opens the doors for trespassers
content: Forest department flags 23 Google Maps listings inside reserve forests in Kozhikode as misleading; officials say unauthorised map pins and uploaded photographs are encouraging illegal entry into protected areas; DFO seeks removal of the listings, warning of trespassing, public safety risks and human-wildlife conflict

title: Bihar CM opens ₹130.88-cr. link to divert floodwaters, enhance farm productivity
content: The link, part of a river interlinking project, will reduce intensity of floods in northern Bihar, says Choudhary; he also inaugurates 551 schools, lists measures to improve education infrastructure

title: At least eight dead, 15 hurt in Nagaland landslide
content: Heavy incessant rainfall and a cloudburst caused widespread destruction at various locations in and around Mon town; State announces ex-gratia of ₹4 lakh for kin of deceased, ₹74,000 for each of the injured

title: Students must unite against drugs: Minister Bindu Krishna
content: She was presiding over the distribution ceremony of the ‘Focus Kollam’ Minister’s Excellence Award-2026, organised for talented students from the Kollam constituency

title: Canada Defies UN Advice, Bans Visitors From Congo Amid Ebola Outbreak
content: The ban contravenes advice from the World Health Organization, which advises against travel or trade restrictions with Congo, where officials are currently struggling to contain a large outbreak of Ebola.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Opens With Sloganeering, Chaos, Adjournments
content: PM Modi's message that "every voice should find an opportunity to be heard", it appears, was lost, as both the Houses of parliament were adjourned shortly after reconvening on the first day of the Session.

title: Zero Electricity Bill? BSES Explains How Delhi Homes Can Save Up To Rs 1 Lakh
content: According to BSES, a 1-kilowatt rooftop solar system can generate around 4 units of electricity every day, and reduce power bills by 50% or more.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Is Sharad Pawar Planning To Merge NCP With NDA? Behind The Ongoing Calculations
content: The NCP patriarch, turning 86 this year, will have to take a decision soon - before someone else takes it for him.

title: Video: Lathicharge By Cops, Chaos Ahead Of CJP's March To Parliament
content: CJP Parliament March: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) supporters had announced a "Chalo Sansad" march against alleged irregularities in NEET.

title: Boy Drowns In Well Of ASI-Protected Karnataka Temple, Locals Flag No Barricades
content: The nine-year-old boy had gone to the Kalleshwara Temple in Karnataka's Vijayanagara with his parents.

title: CJP Protest In Delhi LIVE: Mobile Internet Shut In Parts Of Central Delhi Ahead Of CJP’s Parliament March
content: CJP Parliament March in Delhi LIVE Updates: The police had denied the Cockroach Janta Party the permission to hold the protest march and banned large gatherings.

title: PM Modi's Dig At '56-Year-Old Youth' Rahul Gandhi Ahead Of Monsoon Session
content: PM Modi, while praising the Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace for successfully launching Vikram-1, the country's first privately developed orbital rocket, said on Monday that the feat was achieved by youngsters with an average age of 28 years and not by a "56-year-old youth".

title: Weeks After IT Bloodbath Over Results, Accenture Overhauls Salary Hikes
content: Under new model, half of approved salary hike will be added to an employee's base pay. The remaining half will be paid as a one-time lump-sum amount.

title: Pakistan Turns To Terror Playbook Amid Taliban Troubles, Crisis In PoK
content: Beijing has reportedly shared its concerns about the vulnerability of their personnel and projects at the highest levels in Pakistan, putting Army Chief Munir under serious pressure to deliver results.

title: Woman Loses Way In Forest After Escaping Rape Bid, Cops Rescue Using Drones
content: The woman had taken a lift on a motorcycle from Khurda bus stand. The rider took her towards the Ratanpur Reserve Forest to sexually assault her.

title: PM Modi To Inaugurate Bhogapuram Airport In Andhra On August 1
content: Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu said the airport will transform north Andhra's economic landscape and improve its socio-economic conditions.

title: US Immigration Agents To Wear Body Cameras During Vehicle Stops
content: ICE will require officers to wear body cameras during vehicle stops after two fatal shootings in Texas and Maine sparked public backlash

title: Large Gatherings Banned, Cops Geared Up: Delhi's Plan For CJP's Parliament March
content: The Delhi Police have banned large gatherings in New Delhi district amid CJP's protest plan on a day the monsoon session of parliament will begin

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Conducts Strikes On Iran For 9th Straight Night After Another Soldier Killed
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: CENTCOM said the raids were designed to "swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night."

content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices jump 3% as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel.

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. title: Monsoon Session begins today; Oppn set to target Centre over Ram temple row, NEET, E20
content: the stage is set for a stormy monsoon session of parliament from today . opposition leaders pushed for discussions on the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple, the NEET paper leak, the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and the E20 petrol row . the government cautioned its rivals that any disruption of the legislature is bound to cost them politically .

title: Planet 48 light-years away has 3 key ingredients scientists seek for life
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
